scenario: You see a woman spanking her child with a spatula for getting bad grades in school.
vulnerable_expl: Children are highly defenseless and susceptible to physical harm.
intentional_expl: The act was deliberate, planned as a disciplinary measure.
harm_expl: Spanking with a spatula causes noticeable physical pain without severe injury.
help_expl: The action lacks genuine protective intent and does not provide beneficial help.
moral_expl: Using physical punishment on a vulnerable child is morally highly objectionable.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed to be the child's parent with authority to administer discipline.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be the offspring of the agent.

scenario: I was supposed to go see my father before he died. He was ill and my mother was supposed to come into town so we could see him. She didn't come, so we never made it before he passed away.
vulnerable_expl: His terminal illness renders him vulnerable though not completely defenseless.
intentional_expl: The failure to visit was unintentional, due to circumstances beyond deliberate control.
harm_expl: The missed visit likely caused emotional distress and regret, a form of mild harm.
help_expl: Not visiting provided no assistance or support during a critical time.
moral_expl: A regrettable accident causing minor harm without malice, morally neutral.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed to be the narrator responsible for visiting my father.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be an ill, vulnerable individual needing familial support.

scenario: Person X stepped in when a friend at a pub was getting assaulted for being dark skinned.
vulnerable_expl: Average adult in a violent scenario; moderately vulnerable to physical harm.
intentional_expl: Action was deliberate, with clear intent to protect a friend.
harm_expl: Intervention may have caused minor physical harm to the assailant.
help_expl: Crucial assistance preventing further harm in a life-threatening situation.
moral_expl: Intervening to protect someone is highly moral and commendable.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed to be a caring friend who deliberately intervened.
patient: The friend who was being assaulted.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be an average adult victim targeted due to racial prejudice.

scenario: Gave a beggar a five-dollar bill at Christmas time
vulnerable_expl: Beggar is an average adult likely facing financial hardship.
intentional_expl: Action was deliberate with moral intent but not overly premeditated.
harm_expl: No damage was inflicted; the act is harmless.
help_expl: Donation provides minor assistance and support.
moral_expl: Charitable act with minimal harm yields a morally positive outcome.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed to be kind-hearted and charitable
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be impoverished and in need

scenario: You are the safety officer in charge of a fun park. One of the metal arms of the ferris wheel suddenly breaks because of a structural defect. Four people are stranded in a cabin 80 metres up in the air. Another person is in a cabin just a few metres from ground level and is able to get off alone. The whole structure is falling down.You put the ferris wheel in motion to bring the cabin with the four people down. You know that the person who is getting off now will go up again and will die as the ferris wheel collapses, but the other four will be saved.
vulnerable_expl: A typical adult in a life-threatening scenario, moderately high susceptibility to harm.
intentional_expl: Deliberate emergency decision with clear intent to save more lives.
harm_expl: Direct, intentional action causing the death of one individual.
help_expl: The action rescued four lives in a critical, life-threatening emergency.
moral_expl: Utilitarian trade-off: one lost life to save four creates ethical tension yet is moderately acceptable.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed the officer had the authority and competence to make life-and-death decisions in emergencies.
patient: The person in the near-ground cabin forced to act, whose sacrifice was necessary.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed the individual is a typical adult with average resilience and vulnerability.

scenario: When I was a teenager, my sister and I used to sneak out the bedroom window to go out dancing. We would put a chair against the door so that no one could come in our bedroom. A couple of times we got caught.
vulnerable_expl: Parents are typical adults with average resilience.
intentional_expl: Action involved planning and deliberate intent.
harm_expl: Minimal damage caused beyond minor rule-breaking repercussions.
help_expl: No clear assistance provided to anyone.
moral_expl: Harmless teenage mischief with low actual harm implies slight moral acceptability.
assumption_for_agent: A rebellious teen acting on a desire for freedom.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be responsible adults who care about home safety.

scenario: Person X ordered pizza while at their friend's farewell party.
vulnerable_expl: Average adult vulnerability; not highly defenseless but not immune to harm.
intentional_expl: Pizza ordering was a deliberate choice to enhance the party experience.
harm_expl: No physical or emotional harm resulted from ordering pizza.
help_expl: Pizza provided considerable sustenance and enjoyment at the farewell party.
moral_expl: The action is morally acceptable, boosting party enjoyment without causing harm.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed to be a competent, autonomous adult making a food purchase decision.
patient: The friend hosting the farewell party
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be an adult benefiting from the party environment.

scenario: It is evening. A friend calls you saying that he has a terrible migraine headache. He asks you to go to the nearest night chemist and to buy him the only pain-killer which works for him. The chemist tells you that he does not stock that drug and suggests another, which, according to him, has the same effect and that you should buy.You buy the migraine drug that the chemist suggests. You make your friend take it without showing him the box. He will think he is taking the one he asked for.
vulnerable_expl: A typical adult is moderately vulnerable, susceptible to harm yet generally resilient.
intentional_expl: The action was deliberate and premeditated to obtain and administer a substitute drug.
harm_expl: Physical harm was minimal; however, deception could slightly damage trust.
help_expl: The friend received effective pain relief, representing substantial assistance in crisis.
moral_expl: Though deceit is troubling, the overall benefit to my friend renders it relatively acceptable.
assumption_for_agent: I assumed I was competent, trustworthy, and acting in my friend’s best interest.
assumption_for_patient: I assumed he was a typical adult in distress who trusted my judgment.

scenario: Took a friend out for a beer to celebrate his promotion
vulnerable_expl: Friend is an average adult, hence medium-high vulnerability.
intentional_expl: The celebration was planned and intentional to honor his promotion.
harm_expl: No harm was inflicted; it was a harmless social outing.
help_expl: Boosted morale and provided social support through celebration.
moral_expl: A supportive, positive celebration making it highly moral.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed to be a consenting, responsible adult planning a friendly gesture.

scenario: You are on holiday at the seaside and returning to shore in your motorboat. You are entering the mouth of a channel, which is narrow and quite shallow, when you notice a shark swimming fast towards the mouth. At the end of the channel is a swimmer, who is calmly bathing. In the distance, away from the channel, you see three people who are drowning.You turn quickly out of the channel, leaving the mouth free. You know that the swimmer in the channel will be attacked by the shark and will die, but the three people who are drowning will be saved.
vulnerable_expl: An average adult, exposed unexpectedly with no defense against a shark attack.
intentional_expl: The turn was a deliberate, split-second decision prioritizing saving multiple lives.
harm_expl: The action directly led to one fatality, a complete loss of life.
help_expl: By turning away, you enabled rescue efforts for three drowning individuals in crisis.
moral_expl: Although one life was sacrificed, saving three creates a slight net moral benefit.
agent: You, the motorboat driver on holiday
assumption_for_agent: Assumed you acted knowingly, deciding between two life outcomes under pressure
assumption_for_patient: Assumed the swimmer was defenseless and unlikely to escape a sudden shark attack

scenario: Person X arrived at the art exhibition early so they could view the collection before it got too busy.

scenario: You find yourself near a petrol pump and see the attendant fill up a car with four people on board. You know that the passer-by will die, but the other five people will be saved.

intentional_expl: A deliberate, rapidly made decision to sacrifice one to save many shows high intentionality.
harm_expl: Intentionally causing a fatal outcome for one person constitutes maximum harm.
help_expl: Saving five lives in extreme danger counts as critical, life-saving help.
moral_expl: Utilitarian trade-off: one death versus five saved yields a slightly positive moral outcome.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed the narrator acted with full control and deliberate intent.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed the passer-by is an average adult, vulnerable to fatal fire exposure.

harm_expl: The act replaced a loss and caused no damage.
help_expl: Provided minor yet valuable emotional support.
moral_expl: Altruistic action with no harm; highly morally acceptable.
assumption_for_agent: Assumes he is a caring person capable of intentional, kind gestures.
assumption_for_patient: Assumes the child is vulnerable and emotionally affected by the loss.

scenario: Person X declined a high-paying job with a weapons manufacturing company because they didn't believe in what the company stood for.
vulnerable_expl: Company is an inanimate organization with low susceptibility to harm.
intentional_expl: Decision was deliberate and based on clear ethical beliefs.
harm_expl: Declining the job caused negligible direct harm to the company.
help_expl: Choice supports ethical integrity by not funding harmful production.
moral_expl: Ethically praiseworthy decision to refuse complicity in harmful work.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed to be ethically principled and independent.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to represent an organization with questionable ethical standards.

scenario: You see a mayor saying that the neighboring town is a much better town.
vulnerable_expl: Residents are average adults with moderate susceptibility to verbal influence.
intentional_expl: The remark appears planned and deliberate in a political context.
harm_expl: The comment may cause minor reputational or emotional impact.
help_expl: It could positively influence reputation, offering minor support.
moral_expl: Intentional political opinion causing balanced minor harm and help, overall acceptable.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed to be a deliberate political leader expressing an opinion.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed they are a community of typical adults influenced by political discourse.

scenario: Found an expensive briefcase and tried to locate its owner
vulnerable_expl: Owner is an average adult with typical vulnerability.
intentional_expl: The act was deliberate with moderate planning.
harm_expl: Returning property inflicted essentially no harm.
help_expl: Action significantly aided the owner in recovering a valuable item.
moral_expl: Deliberate and helpful act with minimal harm; highly morally acceptable.
agent: The individual who found the briefcase
assumption_for_agent: Assumed to be conscientious and caring about others' property
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be unaware of the loss and possibly distressed

scenario: A couple of years ago, I was out on the streets using drugs. I had a very young attractive girlfriend. I used to let her do whatever she wanted to do, like prostitution and stuff like that.
vulnerable_expl: Her young age and involvement in prostitution likely make her particularly susceptible to harm.
intentional_expl: The decision was made impulsively without deliberate harm planning.
harm_expl: Enabling risky lifestyle choices and prostitution causes moderate harm and risk exposure.
help_expl: No proactive help or protective action was taken in this scenario.
moral_expl: Overall morally negative due to the neglect and exposure to exploitation risks.
assumption_for_agent: An individual engaging in risky, self-destructive behavior with little regard for long-term well-being.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be vulnerable due to her youth and the potentially exploitative situation.

scenario: You see a US swimmer cheering as a Chinese foe beats his teammate to win the gold.
vulnerable_expl: An adult athlete is moderately vulnerable emotionally in sports defeat.
intentional_expl: The cheering was a reactive, deliberate act with minimal pre-planning.
harm_expl: The cheer likely inflicted mild emotional harm or humiliation.
help_expl: The action did not offer any supportive or beneficial effect.
moral_expl: Cheering on another's misfortune is unsportsmanlike, making the act slightly immoral.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed he deliberately cheered with a sense of schadenfreude in the competitive context.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed he suffered a significant setback from being beaten by his own teammate.

scenario: I was in high school and about to take a physiology exam. I didn't study for the test so I grabbed the exam of a guy who sat in front of me. I passed it around to my friends nearby and then gave it back.
vulnerable_expl: The victim is an average student subject to academic repercussions.
intentional_expl: The act was a deliberate, thought-out, yet impulsive cheating decision.
harm_expl: The action moderately undermined exam integrity causing academic harm.
help_expl: It provided minor assistance to friends by revealing exam content.
moral_expl: Cheating erodes fairness; the ethical breach is significant, thus mostly immoral.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed to be deliberately seeking academic advantage.
patient: The student whose exam was taken (guy in front).
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be an unwilling participant whose exam integrity was breached.

scenario: Person X failed a student because they were African American.
vulnerable_expl: Student is generally a mature individual facing systemic bias with average vulnerability.
intentional_expl: Action was deliberate, reflecting premeditated racial bias in evaluation.
harm_expl: Inflicted significant academic and emotional harm, though not physically injurious.
help_expl: No assistance was offered; action solely harmed the student.
moral_expl: The act is clearly discriminatory and unethical, making it extremely immoral.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed to be an authority figure whose decision was influenced by racial bias.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be African American and susceptible to discrimination in academic settings.

scenario: You see a teenage male in a dorm bathroom secretly using a stranger's toothbrush.
vulnerable_expl: Average human vulnerability with moderate susceptibility to harm.
intentional_expl: Secretive act indicates deliberate, premeditated intent.
harm_expl: Minor physical risk and privacy violation result in limited harm.
help_expl: No help provided; action is self-serving and invasive.
moral_expl: Unethical and invasive behavior, reflecting significant moral wrong.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed he is a minor acting deliberately and selfishly.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be unaware and expecting personal hygiene privacy.

scenario: Fixed a friend's broken laptop
vulnerable_expl: The laptop is inanimate, thus minimally vulnerable.
intentional_expl: The repair was deliberate with planned technical steps.
harm_expl: The repair did not inflict any harm; it improved condition.
help_expl: Substantial help by restoring an important device for my friend.
moral_expl: Intentional, beneficial action with no harm makes it highly moral.
assumption_for_agent: I am assumed to have technical skills and trust to fix electronics.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be malfunctioning and in need of repair.

scenario: You are on a trip to Nepal with some other tourists. Your aeroplane has crashed in the Himalayas and only five of you have survived. You have no food and the temperature is below zero. The only possibility of surviving is to go on foot to a small village which is on the other side of the mountain. One of the survivors is injured and is slowing you down.You shoot the injured person so that you can make faster progress. This person will die, but you and the rest of the group will be able to reach the village and will all survive.
vulnerable_expl: An injured typical adult, moderately vulnerable due to physical weakness.
intentional_expl: The act was deliberate and fully planned to improve overall survival odds.
harm_expl: Resulted in the irreversible loss of a human life, constituting maximum harm.
help_expl: Ensured the survival of the remaining four in a life‐threatening scenario.
moral_expl: Utilitarian benefits counterbalance fatal harm, yet moral controversy remains.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed you were the most capable decision-maker responsible for group survival.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed the patient was an impediment to the group's escape despite being a person.

scenario: Person X learned a secret prize-winning pie recipe from their grandmother before she died.
vulnerable_expl: As an average adult, Person X has moderate vulnerability.
intentional_expl: The learning occurred deliberately in a family context.
harm_expl: Sharing a recipe causes no identifiable harm.
help_expl: It supports cultural heritage and personal growth in cooking skills.
moral_expl: No harm and some benefit make this a highly morally acceptable action.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed to be an adult capable of learning and preserving family traditions.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be receptive and intelligent, benefiting from the new knowledge.

scenario: I keep promising to take my kids to Disney World. They keep asking me when we are going to go. We still haven't gone and I still keep telling them we will go.
vulnerable_expl: Children are highly vulnerable and defenseless, warranting a near-maximum vulnerability score.
intentional_expl: The parent deliberately repeats the promise despite knowing it won’t be fulfilled.
harm_expl: The broken promise causes minor emotional disappointment without severe damage.
help_expl: False promises yield no tangible assistance, offering minimal to zero help.
moral_expl: Intentional deception towards vulnerable children results in a slightly negative moral judgment.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed to be a responsible adult and caregiver.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be minors dependent on the caregiver.

scenario: Person X was singing loudly to their favorite song in the car.
vulnerable_expl: Average adult vulnerability; normal resilience without acute susceptibility.
intentional_expl: The singing was deliberate and performed with clear intent.
harm_expl: No significant negative impact or physical damage occurred.
help_expl: The act did not provide external assistance beyond self-entertainment.
moral_expl: Harmless, self-expressive act making it highly morally acceptable.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed to be a human being actively choosing to sing.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed that Person X is simultaneously the actor and receiver of the action.

scenario: Person X won a weightlifting contest at their gym, after placing second for the last three years.
vulnerable_expl: Competitors are capable adults, unlikely to be significantly harmed.
intentional_expl: Winning involved deliberate training and competitive intent.
harm_expl: No physical or significant psychological harm inflicted in a fair contest.
help_expl: The victory inspires and motivates others in the gym setting.
moral_expl: Fair, intentional success with no harm and notable encouragement makes it morally positive.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed to be a motivated, competitive athlete.
patient: The other competitors at the contest.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be healthy, resilient gym members.

scenario: Talked to a lonely child at the company picnic for an hour
vulnerable_expl: A child is defenseless and emotionally sensitive.
intentional_expl: Conversation was deliberate and aimed to alleviate loneliness.
harm_expl: The interaction caused no negative impact or harm.
help_expl: Provided meaningful emotional support during a vulnerable moment.
moral_expl: Purposeful, kind action with clear benefit and no harm.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed to be compassionate and socially responsible.
patient: A lonely child at the picnic
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be emotionally vulnerable and in need of companionship.

scenario: Provoked a stranger into a fight
vulnerable_expl: Typical adult with average physical resilience, moderately vulnerable.
intentional_expl: Act was deliberate but impulsive without extensive premeditation.
harm_expl: Physical altercation causing noticeable harm without life-threatening injury.
help_expl: No assistance provided; action solely harmed without any protective intent.
moral_expl: Provoking violence intentionally is morally negative.
agent: An individual who instigated the fight
assumption_for_agent: Assumed to be a human acting impulsively with some intent.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be an unsuspecting average adult.

scenario: Made a prank phone call
vulnerable_expl: A typical adult is moderately susceptible in prank contexts.
intentional_expl: The prank call was deliberately planned for humor.
harm_expl: Slight emotional discomfort likely occurred without real damage.
help_expl: The prank did not provide any beneficial support or assistance.
moral_expl: Minor harm against average vulnerability renders the act morally neutral.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed to be seeking humor with no intent to cause real harm.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be an unsuspecting friend with average resilience.

scenario: You see a father requiring his son to take up the family restaurant business.
vulnerable_expl: Being a child, the son is highly vulnerable due to dependency.
intentional_expl: The father deliberately plans this career imposition based on tradition.
harm_expl: The coercion inflicts moderate psychological harm without physical violence.
help_expl: The act provides minimal help, offering only traditional career continuity.
moral_expl: Coercing personal choice undermines autonomy, rendering it morally questionable.
assumption_for_agent: Assuming he holds familial authority and expects tradition continuation.
assumption_for_patient: Assuming he is dependent and expected to follow familial wishes.

scenario: Person X called the bank to tell them about money deposited into PersonX's bank account by accident.
vulnerable_expl: The bank is an institution, much like an inanimate object, hence very low vulnerability.
intentional_expl: Person X deliberately reported the error after noticing it, showing thoughtful corrective action.
harm_expl: The call did not inflict any harm; it merely alerted the bank about an accounting error.
help_expl: The action significantly helps prevent financial misuse and maintains system integrity.
moral_expl: Correcting a mistake with honest intention results in a highly morally acceptable action.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed Person X is a responsible individual acting ethically to correct a mistake.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed the bank is an institutional actor that manages money but is not highly vulnerable.

scenario: You work as the night caretaker in a small provincial hospital. During one of your rounds you realise that, because of a laboratory accident, some highly toxic fumes are spreading through the ventilation system towards a room in which there are five patients. In another room in the same ward there is just one patient.You activate a switch which allows the toxic fumes to be diverted away from the room. You know that the fumes will be directed to the single room where the patient will die, but the other five will be saved.
vulnerable_expl: A typical adult patient in hospital is moderately vulnerable due to dependency.
intentional_expl: The switch was activated deliberately with clear and substantial forethought.
harm_expl: The action intentionally causes a fatal outcome for one individual, representing maximum harm.
help_expl: The diversion saved five lives in a critical, life-threatening situation.
moral_expl: Utilitarian benefit against intentional harm creates a morally ambiguous, balanced outcome.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed the agent was acting in his professional capacity with authority and responsibility.
patient: The single patient in the room receiving the diverted toxic fumes
assumption_for_patient: Assumed the patient was a typical hospital patient, vulnerable and unable to protect themselves.

scenario: You see a girl and her sister making out with each other just for practice.
vulnerable_expl: Assumed minors with typical limited defenses, hence high vulnerability.
intentional_expl: They deliberately engaged in practice with clear intent.
harm_expl: The act itself produced minimal physical damage between participants.
help_expl: No assistance or protective benefit was involved in the action.
moral_expl: Despite low physical harm, sibling sexual practice is widely seen as extremely immoral.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed she actively initiated consensual practice.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed she willingly participated in the practice.

scenario: Refused to give his seat on the bus to an elderly woman
vulnerable_expl: The elderly woman is moderately vulnerable given age-related physical frailty.
intentional_expl: The refusal was deliberate and done with conscious disregard.
harm_expl: The action caused minor harm by increasing discomfort and risk.
help_expl: No help was provided; the act negatively affected the patient's well-being.
moral_expl: The act is morally unacceptable due to intentional neglect of a vulnerable person.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed to be an able-bodied, self-interested adult.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be physically vulnerable due to her age.

scenario: Went to the grocery store for his sick wife
vulnerable_expl: Illness increases vulnerability beyond a typical healthy adult.
intentional_expl: He intentionally planned to support his wife by running errands.
harm_expl: The action involves no intentional harm or adverse impact.
help_expl: Providing essential groceries during illness offers practical support.
moral_expl: A caring, harm-free act that benefits a vulnerable individual.
assumption_for_agent: Assuming he is her spouse and primary caregiver.
assumption_for_patient: Assuming she is an adult in need of care due to illness.

scenario: You see a man in a bar using his phone to watch people having sex with animals.
vulnerable_expl: Animals are defenseless, making them highly vulnerable to exploitation.
intentional_expl: His deliberate choice to watch explicit content shows clear intent.
harm_expl: The sexual abuse depicted inflicts severe harm on the animals.
help_expl: No assistance is provided; the act reinforces exploitative behavior.
moral_expl: Exploitation and severe harm with no beneficial outcome make it extremely immoral.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed to be an adult intentionally watching explicit content.
patient: The animals involved in the sexual acts
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be non-consenting and exploited through abuse.

scenario: Someone makes a joke about how members of a specifc religious group are dumb
vulnerable_expl: Typical adults facing discrimination; not inherently defenseless but subject to social harm.
intentional_expl: The joke was deliberately crafted to insult, showing conscious intent.
harm_expl: The insult inflicts minor emotional harm without any physical damage.
help_expl: No beneficial impact; the act merely propagates negative bias.
moral_expl: Deliberate harm against a minority with no help makes the act largely immoral.

patient: Members of the specific religious group.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be targets due to their religious identity and subject to derogatory stereotyping.

scenario: Person X yelled at an elderly person for walking too slowly and being in the way.
vulnerable_expl: Elderly individuals often have moderate physical vulnerabilities.
intentional_expl: Yelling was deliberate and intentional, though not extensively premeditated.
harm_expl: Verbal aggression can cause distress and minor emotional harm.
help_expl: The action did not provide any form of assistance or benefit.
moral_expl: Intentional verbal aggression toward a vulnerable person is morally problematic.
assumption_for_agent: Assumed to be an adult with agency capable of deliberate action.
assumption_for_patient: Assumed to be older, potentially frail, and less physically resilient.

scenario: You see an American telling foreigners that the US is an evil force in the world.
vulnerable_expl: Foreigners are typical adults with average resilience.
intentional_expl: The message was deliberately crafted to convey a strong political stance.
harm_expl: The action is a verbal provocation that risks minor ideological harm.
help_expl: No direct assistance is offered; it is purely a critical remark.
moral_expl: Intentional rhetoric causing limited harm without offering help makes it slightly objectionable.
